source_pr.title: pkg/executor: fix unstable TestIssue20779
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #61353

Problem Summary:
- `TestIssue20779` was unstable because plan selection could change and the join failpoint panic wasn"t triggered deterministically.

### What changed and how does it work?
- Force `t1`/`t2` to `USE INDEX(idx)` so the plan keeps the expected outer order.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)

Test Details:
- `make failpoint-enable && (go test -run ^TestIssue20779$ -count=50 --tags=intest ./pkg/executor/test/jointest; rc=$?; make failpoint-disable; exit $rc)`
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: `TestIssue20779` was unstable because the optimizer could choose a different plan/index-join path and bypass the intended failpoint, so the test sometimes didn't exercise the target code path.
failure_signature: TestIssue20779: failpoint not triggered / assertions fail when a different join plan is chosen
root_cause_explanation: The test relies on a specific index-join execution path to trigger a failpoint, but optimizer/index selection could vary and bypass the intended code path. This PR adds `use index(idx)` hints to force a stable plan so the failpoint is consistently triggered.
fix_pattern: Add `use index(...)` hints to force a stable plan so the test deterministically exercises the intended index-join path/failpoint.
